title: Policy Instruments for High-Growth Enterprises
abstract: Abstract High-Growth Enterprises (HGEs) have received growing interest from entrepreneurship scholars, the business press, and of course policymakers. HGEs can potentially make large contributions to economic growth, which has led to various policy initiatives. This chapter discusses the main topics surrounding policy interventions for HGEs, including a discussion of the various rationales for policy interventions. We next discuss the many areas that relate to possible HGE policy instruments. We then evaluate some previous HGE policy instruments, discuss some that have been successful, and possible areas for improvement. Our main policy suggestions are critical listening to stakeholders by policymakers, identifying the decision points (e.g., growth trigger points) to leverage the impact of growth stimuli, designing incentives in the spirit of up or out dynamics, and enhancing the coordination of HGE policies.
summary_en: What policies actually help high-growth enterprises (HGEs) flourish? Coad, Harasztosi, Pál and Teruel survey the evidence and discuss policy design. HGEs receive growing attention because of their potential macro impact, but policy interventions vary widely in success. The chapter reviews the rationales for HGE-targeted intervention, evaluates several past programmes, and identifies what's worked and what hasn't. Their main policy suggestions: critically listen to stakeholders, identify decision-points (e.g., growth trigger moments) where intervention has biggest leverage, design incentives in the spirit of 'up or out' dynamics rather than blanket support, and improve coordination across HGE policies that often run in parallel without cross-talk.
data_used: Survey chapter synthesising existing empirical literature on HGE policy interventions. References to HGE policy programmes in EU, US, UK, and other contexts. No new empirical contribution.
policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian HGE policy (NIH, ITM, NGM): the paper's recommendations apply to programmes like Vinegre, Magyar Falu, Modern Városok startup-support tracks, and KKV-stratégia targeting. The 'up-or-out' framing and growth-trigger leverage point are particularly applicable to the Hungarian programme-design debate. Useful for evaluating effectiveness of past HGE programmes.
